title: Operating Cash Flows — Direct Method
event: Ortiz Co. reports sales of $300,000 and cost of goods sold of $180,000. During the year accounts receivable increased $12,000, inventory increased $6,000, and accounts payable increased $9,000. Compute cash received from customers and cash paid to suppliers (direct method).

computationPaths.narration: Direct method converts each income-statement line to its cash twin. Receivable growth means some sales weren't collected; payable growth means some purchases weren't paid.

memorize.body: Cash from customers = Sales − AR increase (+ AR decrease). Cash to suppliers = COGS + Inventory increase − AP increase.
Two hops for suppliers: COGS → purchases (inventory change), purchases → cash (payable change). Skipping a hop is the standard error.

questions.prompt: Sales $300,000, AR increased $12,000. Cash received from customers = ?

distractors.feedback: You added the AR increase. Growing receivables mean sales OUTRAN collections: 300,000 − 12,000.
That's accrual sales. The direct method wants CASH collected — adjust for the receivable change.
